{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Guinea-Bissau women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Guinea-Bissau
| Badge              = 
| Nickname           = ''[[African wild dog|Djurtus]]'' <br> {{lang|pt|Os Dromedários}} (The [[Dromedary|Dromedaries]])
| Association        = [[Football Federation of Guinea-Bissau|Federação de Futebol da Guiné-Bissau]] (FFGB)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Emiliano Té]]
| Captain            = [[Sori Mané]]
| Most caps          = [[Jonas Mendes]] (60)
| Top scorer         = [[Nando Có]] (9)
| Home Stadium       = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]<br />[[Estádio Lino Correia]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = GNB
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|GNB}}
| FIFA max           = 68
| FIFA max date      = November 2016–January 2017
| FIFA min           = 195
| FIFA min date      = February–March 2010
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Guinea-Bissau}}
| Elo max            = 99
| Elo max date       = 11 February 1984
| Elo min            = 159
| Elo min date       = 23 April 2000, 10 June 2001
| pattern_la1        =
| pattern_b1         = _guib2122h
| pattern_ra1        =
| pattern_sh1        = _guib2122h
| pattern_so1        = _guib2122h
| leftarm1           = FF0000
| body1              = FF0000
| rightarm1          = FF0000
| shorts1            = FF0000
| socks1             = FF0000
| pattern_la2        =
| pattern_b2         =_guib2122a
| pattern_ra2        =
| pattern_sh2        =
| pattern_so2        = _guib2122a
| leftarm2           = 03AB68
| body2              = 03AB68
| rightarm2          = 03AB68
| shorts2            = 03AB68
| socks2             = 03AB68
| pattern_la3        =
| pattern_b3         = _guib2122t
| pattern_3a2        =
| pattern_sh3        =
| pattern_so3        =
| leftarm3           = FFF200
| body3              = FFF200
| rightarm3          = FFF200
| shorts3            = FFF200
| socks3             = FFF200
| First game         = {{nowrap|{{fb|GAM|colonial|name=British Gambia}} 1–3 '''Portuguese Guinea''' {{flagdeco|POR}} <br> ([[British Gambia]]; June 2, 1952)}}
| Largest win        = {{fb|GNB}} 7–2 {{fb-rt|BEN}}<br /> ([[Bamako]], [[Mali]]; 3 November 2001)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|GUI}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|GNB}}<br /> ([[Conakry]], [[Guinea]]; 22 July 2017)
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 4
| Regional cup first = [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]], [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]], [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]] and [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]])
}}

[[File:Joueur ivoirien et guinéen 10 (Mauro Rodrigues).jpg|thumb|100px|[[Mauro Rodrigues]] played numerous matches for the Guinea-Bissau national football team]]

The '''Guinea-Bissau national football team''' ([[Portuguese language|Portuguese]]: Seleção nacional de futebol da Guiné-Bissau) represents [[Guinea-Bissau]] in men's international [[association football]] and it is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Guinea-Bissau]], The team has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]]s but qualified for the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] four times, making their debut in [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]. The team is a member of both [[FIFA]] and [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).

==History==
===World Cup qualifying===
Guinea-Bissau entered their first FIFA World Cup qualification with the aim of reaching the [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998 World Cup]] in [[France]]. The first round of [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|African qualification]] required for them to play [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] in a two-legged match. The first leg was held at home in the [[Estadio 24 de Setembro]] in [[Bissau]] on 1 June 1996 with an attendance of 15,000. Guinea-Bissau went 2–0 up at half-time after strikes from [[Pereira Tavares]] in 11th and 36th minutes. Guinea's [[Titi Camara]] equalised with his own brace in the 53rd and 54th minutes. Guinea-Bissau's [[Co Cipriano]] won the game with a 60th-minute penalty to make it 3–2.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.allworldcup.narod.ru/1998/PROTOCOL/QUALIF/CAF/GNBVSGUI.HTM |title=CAF&nbsp;– 1998 FIFA WORLD CUP PRELIMINARIES |publisher=Allworldcup.narod.ru |date=1996-06-01 |access-date=2012-04-20 |archive-date=2018-01-15 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180115014639/http://allworldcup.narod.ru/1998/PROTOCOL/QUALIF/CAF/GNBVSGUI.HTM |url-status=live }}</ref> The away leg was held at the [[Stade du 28 Septembre]] in [[Conakry]]. [[Momo Soumah]] of Guinea scored to level the tie at half-time before [[Nando Có]] scored a bicycle kick for Guinea-Bissau to put his side 4–3 up on aggregate, however another goal from [[Momo Soumah]] and a winner from [[Tibou Bangoura]] won the match for Guinea, giving them a 5–4 lead on aggregate.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.allworldcup.narod.ru/1998/PROTOCOL/QUALIF/CAF/GUIVSGNB.HTM |title=CAF&nbsp;– 1998 FIFA WORLD CUP PRELIMINARIES |publisher=Allworldcup.narod.ru |date=1996-06-16 |access-date=2012-04-20 |archive-date=2016-07-03 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160703165314/http://www.allworldcup.narod.ru/1998/PROTOCOL/QUALIF/CAF/GUIVSGNB.HTM |url-status=live }}</ref>

Guinea-Bissau had never progressed beyond the first round of qualification until the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2022 qualifiers]] where they defeated [[São Tomé and Príncipe national football team|São Tomé and Príncipe]] 3–1 over two legs, thanks to three goals from [[Joseph Mendes]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/news/the-week-in-numbers-x6289|title=World Football: The Week in Numbers|website=FIFA.com|publisher=Fédération Internationale de Football Association|date=13 September 2019|access-date=30 March 2021|archive-date=14 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210614070300/https://www.fifa.com/news/the-week-in-numbers-x6289|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Africa Cup of Nations===
Guinea-Bissau first participated in AFCON qualification in [[1994 African Cup of Nations qualification|1994]] where they defeated [[Cape Verde national football team|Cape Verde]] over three legs, but failed to register a single point in the second round as their draw against [[Togo national football team|Togo]] was annulled after Togo withdrew from the competition. In 1996 Guinea-Bissau withdrew from qualification after a draw and two losses to begin the campaign, leading to their ban for 1998 AFCON.

Guinea-Bissau next entered qualification in 2006 where they lost 4–1 to [[Mali national football team]] and were again banned in 2008 for unpaid debts to [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]. In 2012 they secured a single win (1–0 against [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]]) and five losses to finish last in [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations qualification#Group J|Group J]]. The following year they were eliminated 2–0 by [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]]. In 2015, after beating [[Central African Republic national football team|Central African Republic]] 3–1, they lost to [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]] by the same scoreline.

For the [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2017 qualifiers]], Guinea-Bissau was drawn from Pot 4 into Group E and, despite being the lowest ranked nation in their group, achieved ten points and qualified for the 2017 tournament ahead of [[Congo national football team|Congo]], [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]] and [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]]. In their African Cup of Nations debut, Guinea-Bissau drew 1–1 with [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]] with a 91st-minute equaliser by [[Juary Soares]]. This was the only point they collected at the tournament and they were eliminated in the group stage.

Guinea-Bissau then also won their Group in the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2019 qualifiers]] to reach a second straight [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|Finals]]. They again managed one draw (0–0 against [[Benin national football team|Benin]]) and two losses and failed to progress to the knock-out stages.

On March 30, 2021, Guinea-Bissau went into their final [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification Group I|qualifying match]] against [[Congo national football team|Congo]], needing a victory to qualify. They ended comfortable winners with goals from [[Piqueti]], [[Frédéric Mendy (footballer, born 1988)|Frédéric Mendy]] and [[Jorginho (footballer, born 1995)|Jorginho]] to secure their third successive AFCON appearance.

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Guinea-Bissau national football team results (2020&ndash;present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 6 June
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Bienvenue Kanakimana|Kanakaiama]] {{goal|51}}
|stadium    =
|location   = TBD, Morocco
|attendance =
|referee    =
|report     = https://www.besoccer.com/match/seleccion-guinea-bisau/seleccion-burundi/2026690/lineups
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 9 June
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score      = 0–2
|team2      = {{fb|GAB}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Shorla Aboghe|Aboghe]] {{goal|25}}
*[[Bryan Meyo|Meyo]] {{goal|90+4}}
|stadium    =
|location   = TBD, Morocco
|attendance =
|referee    =
|report     = https://www.besoccer.com/match/seleccion-guinea-bisau/seleccion-gabon/2026687/events
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 4 September
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score      = 1–1
|team2      = {{fb|SLE}}
|goals1     =
*[[Mama Baldé|Baldé]] {{goal|74}}
|goals2     =
*[[Kei Kamara|K. Kamara]] {{goal|45+1}}
|stadium    = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]
|location   = [[Bissau]], Guinea-Bissau
|attendance =
|referee    = Clement Franklin Kpan ([[Ivorian Football Federation|Ivory Coast]])
|report     = https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2025/09/04/africa/wc-qualifying-africa/guinea-bissau/sierra-leone/4236769/lineups/
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 8 September
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|DJI}}
|goals1     =
*[[Iano Imbeni|Imbeni]] {{goal|54}}
*[[Fali Candé|Candé]] {{goal|82}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Estádio 24 de Setembro]]
|location   = [[Bissau]], Guinea-Bissau
|attendance =
|referee    = Aklesso Gnama ([[Togolese Football Federation|Togo]])
|report     = https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2025/09/08/africa/wc-qualifying-africa/guinea-bissau/djibouti/4236773/
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 8 October
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ETH}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
|goals1     =
*[[Ramkel James|James]] {{goal|27}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
|location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
|attendance =
|referee    = Jelly Alfred Chavani ([[South African Football Association|South Africa]])
|report     = https://it.soccerway.com/partita/etiopia-4AGUPqC2/guinea-bissau-0rJs9H8F/?mid=vaB96XIf
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group A|2026 FIFA WC Qualifier]]
|date       = 12 October
|time       = {{UTZ|21:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|EGY}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
|goals1     =
*[[Mohamed Hamdy (footballer, born 1995)|Hamdy]] {{goal|10}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Cairo International Stadium]]
|location   = [[Cairo]], Egypt
|attendance = 41,000
|referee    = Daniel Nii Ayi Laryea ([[Ghana Football Association|Ghana]])
|report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/egypt-bejDn7NN/guinea-bissau-0rJs9H8F/summary/lineups/?mid=EH3M3V2D
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Friendly|Friendly]]
|date       = 18 November
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|ANG}}
|score      = Canceled
|team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|stadium    = TBD
|location   = TBD
|attendance =
|referee    =
|report     = 
|result     = P
}}

===2026===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 28 March
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 5–0
| report     = https://it.soccerway.com/partita/burkina-faso-bJOXzWga/guinea-bissau-0rJs9H8F/riassunto/formazioni/?mid=SlnvlYKj
| team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Pierre Landry Kaboré|P. Kaboré]] {{goal|26}}
*[[Dango Ouattara|Ouattara]] {{goal|40}}
*[[Moïse Elohim Kaboré|M. Kaboré]] {{goal|61||73||88}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    = Moussa Ahamadou Alou ([[Nigerien Football Federation|Niger]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 31 March
| time       = {{UTZ|18:00|0}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|BFA}}
| score      = 1–1
| report     = https://it.soccerway.com/partita/burkina-faso-bJOXzWga/guinea-bissau-0rJs9H8F/?mid=fevQByDc
| team2      = {{fb|GNB}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Adamo Nagalo|Nagalo]] {{goal|85}}
| goals2     = 
*[[Fuscano Gomes|F. Gomes]] {{goal|90+7}}
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], Burkina Faso
| attendance = 
| referee    =
| result     = D
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MAD}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Guinea-Bissau
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MAD}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|GNB}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Madagascar
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TAN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Guinea-Bissau
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|GNB}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Tanzania
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|GNB}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Nigeria
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|NGA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Guinea-Bissau
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Head coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|POR}} [[Luis Boa Morte]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Assistant coaches
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Domingos Indjai<br>{{flagicon|GNB}} Filipe Sambu
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Fitness coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Mustafá Danfa
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Goalkeeper coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Augusto Yansané
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Team doctors
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Dr. Raimundo Djabaté<br>{{flagicon|GNB}} Dr. Francisco Djoró
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Physiotherapists
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Leopoldo Kanté<br>{{flagicon|GNB}} Vasco Saidú<br>{{flagicon|GNB}} Vítor Djaló<br>{{flagicon|GNB}} Gilson Baldé 
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Match analyst
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|GNB}} Lucrécio Sané
|-
|}

===Coaching history===
{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Guilherme Farinha]] (1990–1994)
* {{flagicon|GNB}} [[Armando Antonio Miranda]] (2000)
* {{flagicon|GNB}} [[Baciro Candé]] (2001–2009)
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Luís Norton de Matos]] (2010–2012)
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Carlos Manuel]] (2012–2014)
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Paulo Torres]] (2014–2016)
* {{flagicon|GNB}} [[Baciro Candé]] (2016–2024)
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Luís Boa Morte]] (2024–2025)
* {{flagicon|GNB}} [[Emiliano Té]] (2025–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the [[Exhibition match|friendly]] matches against [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]] on 28 and 31 March.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1201869825307132&set=a.609823681178419https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1318775583616555&set=a.609823681178419|title=JOGO AMIGÁVEL: EMILIANO TÉ ANUNCIA LISTA DE 22 CONVOCADOS PARA DEFRONTAR BURKINA FASO |website=[[Facebook]] |date=26 March 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 27 March 2026, after the first match against [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]]'' <!-- ATTENTION: Substitutes for friendly match against Burkina Faso of 27 March 2026 are NOT known-->

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Manuel Baldé]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|11|14|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[F.C. Penafiel|Penafiel]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Aureliano Lopes Martins]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Le Mée Sport|Le Mée]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Leo Mendes]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|12|8|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Salamanca CF UDS|Salamanca]]|clubnat=SPA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sori Mané]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|4|3|df=y}}|caps=49|goals=0|club=[[Académico de Viseu F.C.|Académico Viseu]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Opa Sanganté]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|2|1|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=0|club=[[USL Dunkerque|Dunkerque]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Iano Imbeni]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|2|2|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[F.C. Penafiel|Penafiel]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Braima Mané Piqué]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|1|22|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Boavista FC (Cape Verde)|Boavista Praia]]|clubnat=CPV}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Etivaldo Mendes]]|age=<!-- {{birth date and age|2005|2|16|df=y}} -->|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bura Nogueira]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|12|22|df=y}}|caps=38|goals=2|club=[[União de Santarém|Santarém]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Carlos Mané]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|3|11|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=2|club=[[Kayserispor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Panutche Camará]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|2|28|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Dundee United|Dundee United]]|clubnat=SCO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Renato Nhaga]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|3|23|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Galatasaray S.K. (football)|Galatasaray]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Edson Silva (footballer, born 2001)|Edson Silva]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|5|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Enosis Neon Paralimni FC|Paralimni]]|clubnat=CYP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Fuscano Gomes]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|2|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Henry Lamine|Henry M. Lamine]]|age=<!-- {{birth date and age|2005|2|16|df=y}} -->|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mauro Rodrigues]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|4|15|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=2|club=[[Yverdon-Sport FC|Yverdon-Sport]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Tamble Monteiro]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|11|15|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Portimonense S.C.|Portimonense]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Elves Baldé]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|10|2|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[S.C. Farense|Farense]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Álvaro Djaló]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|16|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Al-Gharafa SC|Al-Gharafa]]|clubnat=QAT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Flávio Silva]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|4|3|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Kaizer Chiefs F.C.|Kaizer Chiefs]]|clubnat=SAF}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[José Jo]]|age=<!-- {{birth date and age|2005|2|16|df=y}} -->|caps=0|goals=0|club=|clubnat=}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Alassan Manjam]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|9|12|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[CD Tenerife|Tenerife]]|clubnat=SPA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Guinea-Bissau in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Fernando Embadje]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|7|6|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[CD Lugo|Lugo]]|clubnat=SPA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ricardino Té]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Sport Bissau e Benfica|Benfica de Bissau]]|clubnat=GNB|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Manuel Issufe Djaló]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|8|4|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Koeppchen Wormeldange|Koeppchen]]|clubnat=LUX|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nanu (footballer)|Nanu]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|5|17|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=0|club=[[APOEL FC|APOEL]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Fali Candé]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|24|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=1|club=[[US Sassuolo|Sassuolo]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jefferson Encada]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|4|17|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=0|club=[[Pharco FC|Pharco]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Víctor Rofino]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|7|28|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[U.D. Leiria|União Leiria]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Gilberto Batista]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|29|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Moreirense F.C.|Moreirense]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Adramane Cassamá]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|1|16|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[AC Oulu|Oulu]]|clubnat=FIN|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Elydjah Mendy]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|3|14|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Nancy Lorraine|Nancy]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mário Junior]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|4|9|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[U.D. Oliveirense|Oliveirense]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|DJI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sambinha]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|9|23|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Maccabi Bnei Reineh F.C.|Maccabi Bnei Reineh]]|clubnat=ISR|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sene Camara]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|2|25|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Sport Bissau e Benfica|Benfica de Bissau]]|clubnat=GNB|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Augusto Dabó]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|3|13|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Boavista F.C.|Boavista]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
<!-- 
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Babacar Fati]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|2|1|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Seinäjoen Jalkapallokerho|SJK]]|clubnat=FIN|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Saná Gomes (footballer, born 1999)|Saná Gomes]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|10|10|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Omonia Aradippou]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}} 
-->
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dálcio]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|5|22|df=y}}|caps=29|goals=0|club=[[APOEL FC|APOEL]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Zidane Banjaqui]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|12|15|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=2|club=[[Panserraikos F.C.|Panserraikos]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mamadi Camará (footballer, born 2003)|Mamadi Camará]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|31|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Reading F.C.|Reading]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nito Gomes|Nito Gomes Lacerda]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|2|3|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[F.C. Paços de Ferreira|Paços Ferreira]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ronaldo Vieira (footballer, born 1998)|Ronaldo Vieira]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|7|19|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[San Jose Earthquakes]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dai Balde]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|10|18|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Rio Ave F.C.|Rio Ave]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Vando Félix]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|9|3|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Vojvodina|Vojvodina]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
<!-- 
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Alfa Semedo]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|8|30|df=y}}|caps=35|goals=2|club=[[Neom SC|Neom]]|clubnat=KSA|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Moreto Cassamá]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|2|16|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=0|club=[[AC Omonia|Omonia]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jorginho (footballer, born 1995)|Jorginho]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|9|21|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=5|club=[[Omonia Aradippou]]|clubnat=CYP|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Janio Bikel]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|6|28|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Gaziantep F.K.|Gaziantep]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|BUR}}, 24 March 2025}} 
-->
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mama Baldé]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|11|6|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=7|club=[[Stade Brestois 29|Brest]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Franculino Djú]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|6|28|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=1|club=[[FC Midtjylland|Midtjylland]]|clubnat=DEN|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Beto (footballer, born 1998)|Beto]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|31|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Everton F.C.|Everton]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Marciano Tchami|Marciano]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|03|03|df=yes}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[UD Almería|Almería]]|clubnat=ESP|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Marculino Ninte]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|7|1|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[C.D. Mafra|Mafra]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|EGY}}, 12 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jardel (footballer, born 1997)|Jardel]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|9|20|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[Hapoel Ironi Kiryat Shmona F.C.|Ironi Kiryat Shmona]]|clubnat=ISR|latest=v. {{fb|DJI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Zé Turbo]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|10|22|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=1|club=[[FC Pari Nizhny Novgorod|Pari NN]]|clubnat=RUS|latest=v. {{fb|GAB}}, 9 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
''<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.''<br/>
''<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.''<br/>
''<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.''<br/>
''<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.''<br/>
''<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.''<br/>
''<sup>WD</sup> Withdrew from squad.''<br/>
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|12 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Guinea-Bissau |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/79/Guinea_Bissau.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=2022-06-18 |archive-date=2023-07-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230713112347/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/79/2023/Guinea_Bissau.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Guinea Bissau.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| '''[[Jonas Mendes]]''' || 60 || 0 || 2010–present
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Sori Mané]]''' || 49 || 0 || 2017–present
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Piqueti|Piqueti Djassi]] || 40 || 7 || 2015–2023
|-
| 4 ||align=left| '''[[Bura Nogueira]]''' || 39 || 2 || 2015–present
|-
| 5 ||align=left| '''[[Mama Baldé]]''' || 37 || 7 || 2019–present
|-
| rowspan=2|6 ||align=left| [[Adelino Lopes]] || 36 || 2 || 1994–2001
|-
|align=left| '''[[Alfa Semedo]]''' || 36 || 2 || 2021–present
|-
| 8 ||align=left| '''[[Opa Sanganté]]''' || 35 || 0 || 2020–present
|-
| 9 ||align=left| [[Zezinho (Bissau-Guinean footballer)|Zezinho]] || 34 || 2 || 2010–2019
|-
| rowspan=3|10 ||align=left| '''[[Fali Candé]]''' || 33 || 1 || 2021–present
|-
|align=left| '''[[Jefferson Encada]]''' || 33 || 0 || 2021–present
|-
|align=left| '''[[Nanu (footballer)|Nanú]]''' || 33 || 0 || 2019–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Fernando Manuel Co|Nando Co]] || 9 || 6 || {{#expr:9/6 round 2}} || 1996–2001
|-
| rowspan=2|2 ||align=left| '''[[Mama Baldé]]''' || 7 || 37 || {{#expr:7/37 round 2}} || 2019–present
|-
|align=left| [[Piqueti|Piqueti Djassi]] || 7 || 40 || {{#expr:7/40 round 2}} || 2015–2023
|-
| rowspan=2|4 ||align=left| [[Cícero Semedo]] || 6 || 14 || {{#expr:6/14 round 2}} || 2010–2016 
|-
|align=left| [[Frédéric Mendy (footballer, born 1988)|Frédéric Mendy]] || 6 || 24 || {{#expr:6/24 round 2}} || 2016–2022
|-
| 6 ||align=left| '''[[Jorginho (footballer, born 1995)|Jorginho]]''' || 5 || 29 || {{#expr:5/29 round 2}} || 2018–present
|-
| rowspan=2|7 ||align=left| '''[[Zinho Gano]]''' || 4 || 7 || {{#expr:4/7 round 2}} || 2022–present
|-
|align=left| [[Joseph Mendes]] || 4 || 14 || {{#expr:4/14 round 2}} || 2019–2022
|-
| rowspan=3|9 ||align=left| [[Braima Mané|Yuri]] || 3 || 5 || {{#expr:3/5 round 2}} || 1994–1996
|-
|align=left| [[Basile de Carvalho]] || 3 || 8 || {{#expr:3/8 round 2}} || 2011–2012
|-
|align=left| '''[[Toni Silva]]''' || 3 || 22 || {{#expr:3/22 round 2}} || 2016–present
|-
|}

==Competition records==

===FIFA World Cup===

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|colspan=8 |Part of ''{{fb|Portugal}}''
|colspan=6 |Part of ''{{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
|[[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]] to [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]] and [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 4
| 5
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 0
| 3
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 4
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 0
| 1
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 1
| 2
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 0
| 1	
| 1
| 2
| 4
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||3||3||2||8||12
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}} {{flagicon|MEX}} {{flagicon|USA}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10||2||4||4||8||10
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} {{flagicon|POR}} {{flagicon|ESP}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|KSA}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
! 
!{{Tooltip|0/9|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
! 
! 
! 
! 
!
!
!30
!6
!11
!13
!24
!41
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
!rowspan=40|
!colspan=9|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}} 
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}} 
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}} 
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}} 
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}} 
|-
|{{flagicon|SUD|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]
|rowspan=9 colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|rowspan=9 colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Portugal}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|UAR}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|rowspan=6 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|rowspan=6 colspan=8|'' Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]
|- 
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|8
|2
|0
|8
|6
|20
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Banned for withdrawing in 1996''
|colspan=8|''Banned''
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} {{flagicon|NGA}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|MLI}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]||rowspan=4 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} {{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|6
|1
|0
|5
|2
|8
|- 
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|4
|4
|2
|2
|6
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|GAB}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|rowspan=4|Group stage||14th||3||0||1||2||2||5||6||3||1||2||7||7
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|20th||3||0||1||2||0||4||6||2||3||1||8||7
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|22nd||3||0||1||2||0||3||6||3||0||3||9||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|23rd||3||0||0||3||2||7||6||4||1||1||11||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="1" |''Did not qualify''
|6
|1
|2
|3
|4
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!4/35
!12
!0
!3
!9
!4
!19
!54
!20 
!11 
!29
!54
!70
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Amilcar Cabral Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[1983 Amílcar Cabral Cup|1983]]

==References==

{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category}}
*{{Facebook|ffgbissau}}
*[https://ffgb.gw/ Official website] – Guinea-Bissau Football Federeation
*[https://www.ojogo.pt/d/futebol-africano/50/ O'Jogo: News on Portuguese speaking African football teams]

{{Guinea-Bissau national football team}}
{{Guinea-Bissau national football team managers}}
{{Guinea-Bissau squad 2017 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Guinea-Bissau squad 2019 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Guinea-Bissau squad 2021 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Guinea-Bissau squad 2023 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Football in Guinea-Bissau}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Guinea-Bissau}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Guinea-Bissau National Football Team}}
[[Category:Guinea-Bissau national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
